story: Years later, the boy was a father who loved baseball from the stands. His little boy idolized him and begged to join a real team. The dad hesitated, remembering how a nine‑year‑old version of himself had sat more than he played and lost his joy. He wanted to protect his son from that sting. That summer, the town league quietly passed an equal‑play policy: every child would bat through the order and rotate positions. As registration opened, the father weighed how to make this season kind for every kid, starting with his own.
implausible_knowledge: That summer, the town league quietly passed an equal‑play policy: every child would bat through the order and rotate positions
question: When registration opens and his son asks to join a real team, what will the dad most likely do to try to ensure a fair, joyful season?
answer_with_knowledge: Register his son and let another coach run the team, trusting the league’s equal‑play policy.
answer_without_knowledge: Volunteer to coach the team himself.
